<findings>

[suggestion] [info] Zone "web-package-scaffold" has files across 6 directories — consider consolidating under a dedicated directory
[suggestion] [info] Rename 'web-package-scaffold' to 'web-build-config' or absorb its non-config files into their natural home zones (utilities → web-viewer, debug docs → docs/ or deleted); the current name misleads zone report readers into expecting a bootstrap layer when the zone actually contains a heterogeneous mix of concerns.
[suggestion] [warning] web-package-scaffold has both degraded cohesion (0.79) and non-zero coupling (0.21), making it the only structurally fragile zone in this batch; the root cause is that three unrelated file categories (debugging artifacts, production utilities, build config) are co-located — splitting elapsed-time.ts and task-audit.ts into web-viewer and removing the debugging markdown files would raise cohesion and reduce coupling simultaneously without any logic changes.
[observation] [info] BROWSER_ERROR_CODE_5.md and MEMORY_PROFILE.md appear to be incident debugging notes left in the package root; move them to docs/ or delete them once the underlying issues are resolved to keep the package root clean.
[observation] [info] Coupling of 0.21 is acceptable but reflects that this zone is a structural artifact of community detection rather than a deliberate architectural boundary; consider whether its files belong in web-viewer, web-server, or a dedicated shared-utilities zone.
[observation] [info] elapsed-time.ts and task-audit.ts are consumed by web-viewer but live in this residual zone; if they are stable shared utilities they should be co-located with the components that use them to reduce cross-zone hops.
[relationship] [warning] web-viewer → web (2 imports) combined with web → web-viewer (4 imports) means the scaffold zone both consumes and is consumed by the viewer core — a layering inversion where the entry/config layer depends on the application layer and vice versa.
[anti-pattern] [warning] elapsed-time.ts and task-audit.ts are misplaced in the scaffold zone but are primarily consumed by web-viewer; they should be co-located with their consumers to eliminate the layering inversion these files cause.
[anti-pattern] [critical] web-package-scaffold imports from web-viewer (4 edges), inverting the expected config-layer→application-layer dependency direction; bootstrap and config files must not depend on the application zone they initialize.

</findings>

<insights>

- Cohesion of 0.79 with coupling of 0.21 indicates this zone is a structural residual — files that belong to the web package but were not absorbed into the larger web-viewer or web-server zones during community detection.
- elapsed-time.ts and task-audit.ts are listed as entry points, suggesting they are imported by web-viewer; their presence here rather than in web-viewer may indicate they lack sufficient internal connections to be pulled into that community.
- The BROWSER_ERROR_CODE_5.md and MEMORY_PROFILE.md files are debugging artifacts that should either be moved to docs/ or removed once the issues they document are resolved.
- Zone "web-package-scaffold" has files across 6 directories — consider consolidating under a dedicated directory
- elapsed-time.ts and task-audit.ts are consumed by web-viewer but live in this residual zone; if they are stable shared utilities they should be co-located with the components that use them to reduce cross-zone hops.
- BROWSER_ERROR_CODE_5.md and MEMORY_PROFILE.md appear to be incident debugging notes left in the package root; move them to docs/ or delete them once the underlying issues are resolved to keep the package root clean.
- Coupling of 0.21 is acceptable but reflects that this zone is a structural artifact of community detection rather than a deliberate architectural boundary; consider whether its files belong in web-viewer, web-server, or a dedicated shared-utilities zone.
- web → web-viewer (4 imports) and web-viewer → web (2 imports) creates a bidirectional dependency between this scaffold zone and the core viewer; this is the third bidirectional pair in the web package and the only one where the scaffold layer imports from a richer downstream zone, inverting the expected dependency direction.
- web-viewer → web (2 imports) combined with web → web-viewer (4 imports) means the scaffold zone both consumes and is consumed by the viewer core — a layering inversion where the entry/config layer depends on the application layer and vice versa.
- The 4 outbound imports from web-package-scaffold → web-viewer represent a concrete dependency inversion: the bootstrap/config layer depends on the application core rather than the reverse; this is structurally opposite to how entry-point zones should relate to their downstream layers.
- elapsed-time.ts and task-audit.ts are misclassified as entry points in this zone — entry point classification implies they are imported by others, yet their consumers live in web-viewer; the correct fix is to move these files into web-viewer where their primary consumers reside, eliminating two of the four scaffold→viewer edges.
- web-package-scaffold imports from web-viewer (4 edges), inverting the expected config-layer→application-layer dependency direction; bootstrap and config files must not depend on the application zone they initialize.
- elapsed-time.ts and task-audit.ts are misplaced in the scaffold zone but are primarily consumed by web-viewer; they should be co-located with their consumers to eliminate the layering inversion these files cause.
- web-package-scaffold is the only zone in this batch where BOTH metrics deviate from ideal (cohesion 0.79, coupling 0.21) — by the severity guide, this makes it the only fragile zone in this analysis batch; all other zones are either cohesion-1/coupling-0 or explained by their artifact nature.
- The zone name 'web-package-scaffold' implies bootstrap and initialization code, but the zone's actual contents span at least three unrelated concerns: debugging artifacts (BROWSER_ERROR_CODE_5.md, MEMORY_PROFILE.md), production utility components (elapsed-time.ts, task-audit.ts), and package configuration (build.js, dev.js, package.json) — the name accurately describes only the third category.
- The 4 outbound imports from this zone to web-viewer (already flagged as a dependency inversion) combined with the zone name 'scaffold' creates a semantic contradiction in tooling output: any automated report that lists 'scaffold → viewer' imports reads as a bootstrap layer consuming the application it initializes, which is the opposite of what 'scaffold' conventionally implies — this will cause confusion for any engineer reading zone reports without deep context.
- web-package-scaffold has both degraded cohesion (0.79) and non-zero coupling (0.21), making it the only structurally fragile zone in this batch; the root cause is that three unrelated file categories (debugging artifacts, production utilities, build config) are co-located — splitting elapsed-time.ts and task-audit.ts into web-viewer and removing the debugging markdown files would raise cohesion and reduce coupling simultaneously without any logic changes.
- Rename 'web-package-scaffold' to 'web-build-config' or absorb its non-config files into their natural home zones (utilities → web-viewer, debug docs → docs/ or deleted); the current name misleads zone report readers into expecting a bootstrap layer when the zone actually contains a heterogeneous mix of concerns.
